perturbation.original_text: (a) " Confidential Information " means all information heretofore developed or used by the Seller or any of its Affiliates relating to the Restricted Business (as defined below) operations, employees, customers and clients of the Seller, including, but not limited to, customer and client lists, customer or client orders, financial data, pricing information and price lists, business plans and market strategies and arrangements, all books, records, manuals, advertising materials, catalogues, correspondence, mailing lists, production data, sales materials and records, purchasing materials and records, personnel records, quality control records and procedures included in or relating to the Restricted Business or any of the assets of the Seller, and all trademarks, copyrights and patents and applications therefor, all trade secrets, inventions, processes, procedures, research records, market surveys and marketing know-how and other technical papers. The term "Confidential Information" also includes any other information heretofore or hereafter acquired by the Seller and deemed by it to be confidential.
perturbation.changed_text: (a) " Confidential Information " means all information heretofore developed or used by the Seller or any of its Affiliates relating to the Restricted Business (as defined below) operations, employees, customers and clients of the Seller, including, but not limited to, customer and client lists, customer or client orders, financial data, pricing information and price lists, business plans and market strategies and arrangements, all books, records, manuals, advertising materials, catalogues, correspondence, mailing lists, production data, sales materials and records, purchasing materials and records, personnel records, quality control records and procedures included in or relating to the Restricted Business or any of the assets of the Seller, and all trademarks, copyrights and patents and applications therefor, all trade secrets, inventions, processes, procedures, research records, market surveys and marketing know-how and other technical papers. The term "Confidential Information" also includes any other information heretofore or hereafter acquired by the Seller and deemed by it to be confidential, **excluding any generally known industry practices**.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the phrase 'excluding any generally known industry practices,' this change contradicts the earlier part of the definition of 'Confidential Information,' which broadly includes 'all information' related to the Restricted Business. If information is 'generally known,' it is arguably still 'information' related to the business. This introduces ambiguity and uncertainty about whether the Seller and Guarantor are prohibited from using or disclosing commonly known industry practices, creating conflict within Section 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information. Seller and Guarantor acknowledge that it is the policy of the Buyer to maintain as secret and confidential all Confidential Information, and the parties hereto recognize that Seller and Guarantor have acquired Confidential Information. Seller and Guarantor recognize that all such Confidential Information is and shall remain the sole property of the Buyer, free of any rights of Seller or Guarantor, and acknowledges that the Buyer and its Affiliates have a vested interest in assuring that all such Confidential Information remains secret and confidential. Therefore, the Seller and Guarantor agree that at all times from after the date hereof, they will not, directly or indirectly, without the prior written consent of the Buyer, disclose to any person, firm, company or other entity (other than the Buyer or any of its Affiliates) any Confidential Information, except to the extent that (i) any such Confidential Information becomes generally available to the public or trade, other than as a result of a breach by the Seller or Guarantor of this Section 3, or (ii) any such Confidential Information becomes available to the Seller or Guarantor on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the Seller, Guarantor, Buyer or any of their Affiliates or advisors; provided, that such source is not known by the Seller or Guarantor to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other obligation of secrecy to, the Seller, Guarantor, Buyer or another party. In addition, it shall not be a breach of the confidentiality obligations hereof if the Seller or Guarantor is required by law or legal process to disclose any Confidential Information; provided, that in such case, the Seller or Guarantor shall (a) give the Buyer prompt notice that such disclosure is or may be required, and (b) cooperate with the Buyer, at the Buyer's expense, in protecting, to the maximum extent legally permitted, the confidential or proprietary nature of the Confidential Information which must be so disclosed. The obligations of the Seller and Guarantor under this Section 3 shall survive any termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds "excluding any generally known industry practices" to the definition of 'Confidential Information'. This contradicts Section 3, which requires the Seller and Guarantor to maintain all Confidential Information as secret and confidential. The addition creates a direct conflict because generally known industry practices, although information related to the Restricted Business, are now excluded from the definition of Confidential Information, making it uncertain whether the Seller and Guarantor are prohibited from disclosing or using them, which leads to a contradiction as these practices would arguably still be considered 'information' as defined in Section 3.

perturbation.original_text: (b) The term " control ", with respect to any person, means the power to direct the management and policies of such person, directly or indirectly, by or through stock ownership, agency or otherwise, or pursuant to or in connection with an agreement, arrangement or understanding (written or oral) with one or more other persons by or through stock ownership, agency or otherwise; and the terms "controlling" and "controlled" have meanings correlative to the foregoing.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) The term " control ", with respect to any person, means the power to direct the management and policies of such person, directly or indirectly, by or through stock ownership, agency or otherwise, or pursuant to or in connection with an agreement, arrangement or understanding (written or oral) with one or more other persons by or through stock ownership, agency or otherwise; and the terms "controlling" and "controlled" have meanings correlative to the foregoing. **However, for the purposes of Section 2, 'control' requires explicit written consent from the Buyer**.
perturbation.explanation: This addition introduces an in-text contradiction by requiring 'explicit written consent from the Buyer' for 'control' to be established, but only for the purposes of Section 2 (Non-competition). The original definition of control is much broader and doesn't require explicit written consent. This creates uncertainty regarding what constitutes 'control' outside of Section 2. For example if section 4 has the work 'control', is it interpreted as the normal definintion for 'control' or the added one. The primary contradiction is now within section 2, because there is a different condition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Non-competition. At all times from and after the date of this Agreement and until the expiration of the Restricted Period, Seller and Guarantor shall not: (a) directly or indirectly engage in, be employed by, own, manage, operate, provide financing to, control or participate in the ownership, management or control of, or otherwise have an interest (whether, subject to Section 5, as a stockholder, director, officer, employee, representative, subcontractor, partner, consultant, proprietor, agent or otherwise) in, or cause, authorize, aid or assist any other person to own, manage, operate, provide financing to, control or otherwise have an interest in, any business or any person who is engaged in any business that directly or indirectly competes or intends to compete with the Restricted Business anywhere in the world, unless Seller or Guarantor purchase or own less than five percent (5%) of capital stock in a publicly held company; or (b) directly, indirectly or otherwise by letters, circulars or advertisements, and whether for itself or on behalf of any other person, canvass or solicit or, directly or indirectly, cause or authorize to be solicited, or enter into or effected, or, directly or indirectly, cause or authorize to be entered into or effected, any business or orders for businesses competing with the Restricted Business from any person who (i) at the time of the Agreement or within two years prior to the date of the Agreement, has been, a customer or client, or (ii) is an active prospect to be a customer or client, in each case, of the Seller at the time of the Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an explicit condition ('explicit written consent from the Buyer') for 'control' specifically within Section 2, which directly contradicts the broader, more general definition of 'control' established in the original text. Section 2's non-competition clause uses 'control', and now the definition of 'control' within that section is different from the standard definition of control. This necessitates a different interpretation of 'control' within Section 2, compared to how 'control' is used elsewhere in the agreement. This creates an in-text contradiction as per the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 2. Non-competition. At all times from and after the date of this Agreement and until the expiration of the Restricted Period, Seller and Guarantor shall not: (a) directly or indirectly engage in, be employed by, own, manage, operate, provide financing to, control or participate in the ownership, management or control of, or otherwise have an interest (whether, subject to Section 5, as a stockholder, director, officer, employee, representative, subcontractor, partner, consultant, proprietor, agent or otherwise) in, or cause, authorize, aid or assist any other person to own, manage, operate, provide financing to, control or otherwise have an interest in, any business or any person who is engaged in any business that directly or indirectly competes or intends to compete with the Restricted Business anywhere in the world, unless Seller or Guarantor purchase or own less than five percent (5%) of capital stock in a publicly held company; or (b) directly, indirectly or otherwise by letters, circulars or advertisements, and whether for itself or on behalf of any other person, canvass or solicit or, directly or indirectly, cause or authorize to be solicited, or enter into or effect, or, directly or indirectly, cause or authorize to be entered into or effected, any business or orders for businesses competing with the Restricted Business from any person who (i) at the time of the Agreement or within two years prior to the date of the Agreement, has been, a customer or client, or (ii) is an active prospect to be a customer or client, in each case, of the Seller at the time of the Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Non-competition. At all times from and after the date of this Agreement and until the expiration of the Restricted Period, Seller and Guarantor shall not: (a) directly or indirectly engage in, be employed by, own, manage, operate, provide financing to, control or participate in the ownership, management or control of, or otherwise have an interest (whether, subject to Section 5, as a stockholder, director, officer, employee, representative, subcontractor, partner, consultant, proprietor, agent or otherwise) in, or cause, authorize, aid or assist any other person to own, manage, operate, provide financing to, control or otherwise have an interest in, any business or any person who is engaged in any business that directly or indirectly competes or intends to compete with the Restricted Business anywhere in the world, unless Seller or Guarantor purchase or own less than five percent (5%) of capital stock in a publicly held company; or (b) directly, indirectly or otherwise by letters, circulars or advertisements, and whether for itself or on behalf of any other person, canvass or solicit or, directly or indirectly, cause or authorize to be solicited, or enter into or effect, or, directly or indirectly, cause or authorize to be entered into or effected, any business or orders for businesses competing with the Restricted Business from any person who (i) at the time of the Agreement or within two years prior to the date of the Agreement, has been, a customer or client, or (ii) is an active prospect to be a customer or client, in each case, of the Seller at the time of the Agreement. **However, this clause shall not apply if the Buyer undergoes a Change of Control.**
perturbation.explanation: By adding the phrase 'However, this clause shall not apply if the Buyer undergoes a Change of Control,' an in-text contradiction is introduced. The original clause states that the Seller and Guarantor 'shall not' compete during the Restricted Period. The addition creates an exception that completely negates the original restriction if a Change of Control occurs. This introduces ambiguity regarding the non-compete obligations under specific circumstances, making it unclear when the non-compete actually applies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Non-competition. At all times from and after the date of this Agreement and until the expiration of the Restricted Period, Seller and Guarantor shall not: (a) directly or indirectly engage in, be employed by, own, manage, operate, provide financing to, control or participate in the ownership, management or control of, or otherwise have an interest (whether, subject to Section 5, as a stockholder, director, officer, employee, representative, subcontractor, partner, consultant, proprietor, agent or otherwise) in, or cause, authorize, aid or assist any other person to own, manage, operate, provide financing to, control or otherwise have an interest in, any business or any person who is engaged in any business that directly or indirectly competes or intends to compete with the Restricted Business anywhere in the world, unless Seller or Guarantor purchase or own less than five percent (5%) of capital stock in a publicly held company; or (b) directly, indirectly or otherwise by letters, circulars or advertisements, and whether for itself or on behalf of any other person, canvass or solicit or, directly or indirectly, cause or authorize to be solicited, or enter into or effect, or, directly or indirectly, cause or authorize to be entered into or effected, any business or orders for businesses competing with the Restricted Business from any person who (i) at the time of the Agreement or within two years prior to the date of the Agreement, has been, a customer or client, or (ii) is an active prospect to be a customer or client, in each case, of the Seller at the time of the Agreement. However, this clause shall not apply if the Buyer undergoes a Change of Control.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clause that nullifies the original non-compete agreement under a specific condition (Change of Control). This directly conflicts with the original clause, which states that the Seller and Guarantor 'shall not' compete during the Restricted Period. The addition of the Change of Control exception creates a clear and direct inconsistency, making it unclear when the non-compete obligation applies, thus adhering to the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.'

perturbation.original_text: 3. Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information. Seller and Guarantor acknowledge that it is the policy of the Buyer to maintain as secret and confidential all Confidential Information, and the parties hereto recognize that Seller and Guarantor have acquired Confidential Information. Seller and Guarantor recognize that all such Confidential Information is and shall remain the sole property of the Buyer, free of any rights of Seller or Guarantor, and acknowledges that the Buyer and its Affiliates have a vested interest in assuring that all such Confidential Information remains secret and confidential. Therefore, the Seller and Guarantor agree that at all times from after the date hereof, they will not, directly or indirectly, without the prior written consent of the Buyer, disclose to any person, firm, company or other entity (other than the Buyer or any of its Affiliates) any Confidential Information, except to the extent that (i) any such Confidential Information becomes generally available to the public or trade, other than as a result of a breach by the Seller or Guarantor of this Section 3, or (ii) any such Confidential Information becomes available to the Seller or Guarantor on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the Seller, Guarantor, Buyer or any of their Affiliates or advisors; provided, that such source is not known by the Seller or Guarantor to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other obligation of secrecy to, the Seller, Guarantor, Buyer or another party.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information. Seller and Guarantor acknowledge that it is the policy of the Buyer to maintain as secret and confidential all Confidential Information, and the parties hereto recognize that Seller and Guarantor have acquired Confidential Information. Seller and Guarantor recognize that all such Confidential Information is and shall remain the sole property of the Buyer, free of any rights of Seller or Guarantor, and acknowledges that the Buyer and its Affiliates have a vested interest in assuring that all such Confidential Information remains secret and confidential. Therefore, the Seller and Guarantor agree that at all times from after the date hereof, they will not, directly or indirectly, without the prior written consent of the Buyer, disclose to any person, firm, company or other entity (other than the Buyer or any of its Affiliates) any Confidential Information, except to the extent that (i) any such Confidential Information becomes generally available to the public or trade, other than as a result of a breach by the Seller or Guarantor of this Section 3, or (ii) any such Confidential Information becomes available to the Seller or Guarantor on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the Seller, Guarantor, Buyer or any of their Affiliates or advisors; provided, that such source is not known by the Seller or Guarantor to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other obligation of secrecy to, the Seller, Guarantor, Buyer or another party. **Notwithstanding the foregoing, Guarantor shall be permitted to disclose Confidential Information to its legal counsel without prior written consent.**
perturbation.explanation: The added sentence 'Notwithstanding the foregoing, Guarantor shall be permitted to disclose Confidential Information to its legal counsel without prior written consent' creates a direct in-text contradiction. The original clause mandates that Seller and Guarantor 'will not, directly or indirectly, without the prior written consent of the Buyer, disclose...any Confidential Information.' The addition provides an exception for the Guarantor to disclose to its legal counsel, negating the original restriction for that specific scenario. Now it is unclear whether the 'without prior written consent' applies to everyone else but not the Guarantor's legal counsel, leading to legal challenges.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information. Seller and Guarantor acknowledge that it is the policy of the Buyer to maintain as secret and confidential all Confidential Information, and the parties hereto recognize that Seller and Guarantor have acquired Confidential Information. Seller and Guarantor recognize that all such Confidential Information is and shall remain the sole property of the Buyer, free of any rights of Seller or Guarantor, and acknowledges that the Buyer and its Affiliates have a vested interest in assuring that all such Confidential Information remains secret and confidential. Therefore, the Seller and Guarantor agree that at all times from after the date hereof, they will not, directly or indirectly, without the prior written consent of the Buyer, disclose to any person, firm, company or other entity (other than the Buyer or any of its Affiliates) any Confidential Information, except to the extent that (i) any such Confidential Information becomes generally available to the public or trade, other than as a result of a breach by the Seller or Guarantor of this Section 3, or (ii) any such Confidential Information becomes available to the Seller or Guarantor on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the Seller, Guarantor, Buyer or any of their Affiliates or advisors; provided, that such source is not known by the Seller or Guarantor to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other obligation of secrecy to, the Seller, Guarantor, Buyer or another party. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Guarantor shall be permitted to disclose Confidential Information to its legal counsel without prior written consent.
perturbation.justification: The added sentence in the changed text, 'Notwithstanding the foregoing, Guarantor shall be permitted to disclose Confidential Information to its legal counsel without prior written consent,' directly contradicts the original clause in Section 3. The original clause states that 'the Seller and Guarantor agree that at all times from after the date hereof, they will not, directly or indirectly, without the prior written consent of the Buyer, disclose...any Confidential Information.' This creates a clear in-text contradiction because the Guarantor is now allowed to disclose Confidential Information without prior consent in a specific circumstance (to legal counsel), which is directly prohibited by the earlier part of the same section, creating inconsistencies as defined in the definitions.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Right to Injunctive Relief. Seller and Guarantor acknowledge that any breach or threatened breach by it of any of the covenants or provisions contained herein will result in irreparable and continuing harm to the Buyer for which the Buyer would not have adequate remedy at law. Therefore, Seller and Guarantor acknowledges and agrees that, in addition to any other remedy which the Buyer may have at law or in equity, the Buyer shall be entitled to injunctive relief or other equitable remedies in the event of any such breach or threatened breach. Seller and Guarantor further acknowledges and agrees that monetary damages would be insufficient to compensate the Buyer in the event of a breach by Seller or Guarantor of any of the covenants or provisions contained herein, and that in the event of a breach thereof, the Buyer shall be entitled to specific performance of the obligations hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Right to Injunctive Relief. Seller and Guarantor acknowledge that any breach or threatened breach by it of any of the covenants or provisions contained herein will result in irreparable and continuing harm to the Buyer for which the Buyer would not have adequate remedy at law. Therefore, Seller and Guarantor acknowledges and agrees that, in addition to any other remedy which the Buyer may have at law or in equity, the Buyer shall be entitled to injunctive relief or other equitable remedies in the event of any such breach or threatened breach. Seller and Guarantor further acknowledges and agrees that monetary damages would be insufficient to compensate the Buyer in the event of a breach by Seller or Guarantor of any of the covenants or provisions contained herein, and that in the event of a breach thereof, the Buyer shall be entitled to specific performance of the obligations hereunder. **However, Buyer shall not be entitled to injunctive relief for any breach of Section 4.**
perturbation.explanation: By appending 'However, Buyer shall not be entitled to injunctive relief for any breach of Section 4', a direct in-text contradiction is introduced. The original clause states the Buyer is 'entitled to injunctive relief or other equitable remedies in the event of any such breach or threatened breach'. The added sentence explicitly removes this entitlement for breaches of Section 4 (Non-Solicitation). This creates uncertainty and disagreement in the enforcement of remedies available for a specific section in the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Right to Injunctive Relief. Seller and Guarantor acknowledge that any breach or threatened breach by it of any of the covenants or provisions contained herein will result in irreparable and continuing harm to the Buyer for which the Buyer would not have adequate remedy at law. Therefore, Seller and Guarantor acknowledges and agrees that, in addition to any other remedy which the Buyer may have at law or in equity, the Buyer shall be entitled to injunctive relief or other equitable remedies in the event of any such breach or threatened breach. Seller and Guarantor further acknowledges and agrees that monetary damages would be insufficient to compensate the Buyer in the event of a breach by Seller or Guarantor of any of the covenants or provisions contained herein, and that in the event of a breach thereof, the Buyer shall be entitled to specific performance of the obligations hereunder. However, Buyer shall not be entitled to injunctive relief for any breach of Section 4.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'However, Buyer shall not be entitled to injunctive relief for any breach of Section 4.' to Section 5. This directly contradicts the original statement in Section 5, which states the Buyer 'shall be entitled to injunctive relief or other equitable remedies in the event of any such breach or threatened breach' without excluding any specific section. The contradiction is clear and creates a conflict regarding the availability of injunctive relief for breaches of Section 4.
